com.irurueta.commons.units

Class LocationDistanceEstimator

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.irurueta.commons.units.LocationDistanceEstimator

  • public class LocationDistanceEstimator
extends Object
    Helper class to estimate dinstance between two points expressed in latitude and longitude coordinates in degrees. This method assumes that Earth is a perfect sphere.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static double DEGREES_TO_RADIANS
      Conversion factor to convert degrees to radians.
      static double EQUATORIAL_EARTH_RADIUS
      Earth radius expressed in kilometers.
      static double KILOMETERS_TO_METERS
      Conversion factor of Kilometers to meters.

      Returns distance in kilometers between provided points assuming that Earth is perfectly spherical.
      static double approximateDistanceInKilometers(double latitude1, double longitude1, double latitude2, double longitude2, double radius)
      Computes distance between two points using units of provided radius on a given Spehere.

      Returns distance in kilometers between provided points.

    • Constructor Detail

    • Method Detail

      • distanceInMeters

        public static double distanceInMeters(double latitude1,
                                      double longitude1,
                                      double latitude2,
                                      double longitude2)
        Returns distance in meters between provided points. This method assumes that Earth is an ellipsoid and computes the geodesic distance between provided points.
        Parameters:
        latitude1 - latitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        longitude1 - longitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        latitude2 - latitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        longitude2 - longitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        Returns:
        distance in meters between provided points.

      • distanceInKilometers

        public static double distanceInKilometers(double latitude1,
                                          double longitude1,
                                          double latitude2,
                                          double longitude2)
        Returns distance in kilometers between provided points. This method assumes that Earth is an ellipsoid and computes the geodesic distance between provided points.
        Parameters:
        latitude1 - latitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        longitude1 - longitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        latitude2 - latitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        longitude2 - longitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        Returns:
        distance in kilometers between provided points.

      • approximateDistanceInKilometers

        public static double approximateDistanceInKilometers(double latitude1,
                                                     double longitude1,
                                                     double latitude2,
                                                     double longitude2)
        Returns distance in kilometers between provided points assuming that Earth is perfectly spherical. Computed distances are only accurate for small distances. If more precise (although more expensive to compute) distances are required then use distanceInKilometers(double, double, double, double) instead.
        Parameters:
        latitude1 - latitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        longitude1 - longitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        latitude2 - latitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        longitude2 - longitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        Returns:
        distance in kilometers between provided points.

      • approximateDistanceInKilometers

        public static double approximateDistanceInKilometers(double latitude1,
                                                     double longitude1,
                                                     double latitude2,
                                                     double longitude2,
                                                     double radius)
                                              throws IllegalArgumentException
        Computes distance between two points using units of provided radius on a given Spehere. This method assumes that Earth is perfectly spherical, hence distance computations are only accurate for small distances. If more precise (although more expensive to compute) distances are required then use distanceInKilometers(double, double, double, double) instead.
        Parameters:
        latitude1 - latitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        longitude1 - longitude of 1st point expressed in degrees.
        latitude2 - latitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        longitude2 - longitude of 2nd point expressed in degrees.
        radius - radius of a Sphere.
        Returns:
        distance expressed in same units as provided radius.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if radius is negative.
